w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: is entered and executed on this day of
out of free will and mutual consent
Flat No 403, A Block, MS9 Suma Enclave,
Road, Beside Maruti Home, Kurnool, PO:
(Hereinafter referred as the party of the First part) / (Hereinafter referred as the First Party)
(Hereinafter referred as the party of the Second part) / (Hereinafter referred as the Second Party)
(Hereinafter referred as the party of the
which term shall mean and include their Heirs, Legal, Representatives, Executors,
have expressed their intention to operate the business
in the name and style of

Promoting, owning, running, administering, managing, investing in or establishing hospital, modern hospital, multi-specialty hospital, patient care, Outpatient services, Inpatient services, pharmacies, laboratories, X-Ray, pathological laboratory (Biological and Micro Biology) and other modern diagnostic services either own and/or through franchisees and all other activities incidental to the business or businesses as mutually agreed up on by the partners from time to time
in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations
Now this deed witness is as follows: -
Name and Nature of the Firm
That the business of the Partnership Shall be to

Promoting, owning, running, administering, managing, investing in or establishing hospital, modern hospital, multi-specialty hospital, patient care, Outpatient services, Inpatient services, pharmacies, laboratories, X-Ray, pathological laboratory (Biological and Micro Biology) and other modern diagnostic services either own and/or through franchisees and all other activities incidental to the business or businesses as mutually agreed up on by the partners from time to time
in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations
The name of the firm shall be
The registered office of the business is situated at
That the partnership shall come into effect from the
and shall be continued till it is determined or as mutually decided upon by the partners as per their will.

The necessary capital required for the purpose of business of a partnership firm shall be arranged and contributed by the partners as per the requirement of the business as mutually decided by the partners from time to time.

Interest at the rate of 12% per annum or as may prescribed U/s. 40(b) (iv) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, or any other applicable provision as may be in force in the Income Assessment of the partnership firm for the relevant period shall be payable to the partners on the amount standing to the credit of the account of the partners. Such Interest shall be credited to the account of each partner at the close of the accounting year
Proper Books of Accounts of the Partnership shall be Maintained by

which shall be kept at the Principle Place of Business and would be Opened for Inspection at all Reasonable times to all the Partners who will be free to take copies and the Accounts of the Firm shall be closed by 31st March Every year.

The Remuneration payable to the All Partners as decided by All the Partners from time to time and not to exceed the maximum permissible limit U/s. 40 (b) and other related Provisions under Income-tax Act, 1961 as Amended from time to time.

The Profit and Loss shall be ascertained after deducting all expenses, charges and other expenses after providing for such reserves and making such appropriations as the partners may, on their absolute discretion, think fit.
The Profit and Losses of the firm as ascertained after providing for all expenses shall be shared by the partners on the following ratios:
Party of the First Part namely
shall take an active part in the day-to-day conduct of the business affairs and shall act as representatives on behalf of the firm in dealings with various authorities, including
Income Tax, Goods and Service Tax, Municipal Tax, Electricity authorities,
authorities, to ensure compliance with all necessary statutory requirements related to the business.
: Bank account(s) shall be established in the name of the firm with commercial banks or financial institutions. Party of the First Part namely
authorized to manage and operate the bank account

are authorized jointly to raise any loans and advances on behalf of the firm for the purpose of the business from any Nationalized Banks or from any Financial Institutions or such others and the firm shall be liable for all such loans and advances and the firm shall be binding on all such acts.
All business contracts in respect of the partnership business shall be made in the name of the Firm namely
The Duration of the Partnership Business shall be at will.

That in the event of demise of any partner(s), one person representing the deceased being the eldest male child or, in the alternative, spouse or any other legal heir next in the line would step into the shoes of the deceased and would be taken as partner, to which the surviving partners would have no objection.

Any disputes, differences, or questions arising between the parties, their representatives, or assignees concerning the interpretation or any aspect of this agreement, including rights, duties, or liabilities, shall be resolved through arbitration. This provision constitutes an agreement to submit to arbitration, and the Indian Arbitration Act of 1940 shall govern its implementation
The Partners are at Liberty to Retire from the firm by giving

in writing. The Retiring Partners is not entitled to any share in the Goodwill of the firm. However, the Share of his Investment and Share in Profits till the date of Retirement, failing which the same shall be paid as per the settlement decided by the partners at such times

Any of the above terms may be Altered, Deleted, Modified, Cancelled or Added with Mutual Consent of the Parties herein and the same shall be reduced into Writing on a Stamped Paper worth Rs.100. It is also agreed that such Alterations or Changes or Modifications made shall form part of this Partnership Deed and the Provisions of the Indian
Partnership Act, 1932 shall apply as regards the matter, which are not expressly provided for herein above.
Partners may only withdraw capital with
consent but can freely draw profits annually credited to their respective personal accounts.
The Partnership Business is at will and can be dissolved as and when decided by all the Partners hereto mutually
. On firm dissolution, net assets excluding goodwill are shared among partners after adjusting any partner’s excess investment in the firm.
In witness of the above the said partners put their hands to this deed of partnership on at
